objective: Stabilize RunPod worker startup for qwen/qwen3.5-27b with deterministic nightly packaging and conservative runtime safety defaults.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible, fork-local runtime updates in Dockerfile, src/engine_args.py, and aiwf.request.json: pin vLLM nightly package version, add HF token env aliases, and add Qwen3.5 safety defaults only when users do not explicitly set corresponding env vars.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile adds VLLM_NIGHTLY_VERSION with default 0.16.1rc1.dev141+g792a74b97 and installs that exact nightly version when VLLM_NIGHTLY=true.
Dockerfile stable fallback path remains unchanged when VLLM_NIGHTLY!=true.
src/engine_args.py maps HUGGINGFACE_ACCESS_TOKEN and HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN to hf_token without removing existing env behavior.
src/engine_args.py applies qwen3.5 safety defaults language_model_only=True and enforce_eager=True only when LANGUAGE_MODEL_ONLY / ENFORCE_EAGER are not explicitly provided.
Safety-default application is logged with clear informational messages.
All edits remain fork-local, minimal, and reversible via documented rollback actions.
tests_expected: Run a fork-local RunPod smoke test with qwen/qwen3.5-27b and verify deterministic startup behavior with no EngineCore init regression
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback in this fork: revert Dockerfile nightly pin lines and src/engine_args.py alias/Qwen3.5 safety-default block in one commit; restore previous floating nightly installer behavior and previous engine-arg behavior. No upstream operations required.
deploy_plan: Validate in this fork only with py_compile + JSON validation + docker buildx bake --print + focused diff, then run RunPod smoke test on qwen/qwen3.5-27b. If stable, publish fork release; if regressions appear, execute one-commit rollback.
threat_model: Primary risks are (1) pinned nightly becoming unavailable, and (2) qwen3.5 defaults changing behavior for users relying on multimodal/eager-disabled startup. Mitigations: keep stable branch path untouched, gate safety defaults on explicit env presence so users can override, log every safety-default application, and keep rollback to a single small fork-local commit.
